title: Vault note model + frontmatter round-trip I/O
description: Create a new src/vault/ module with a typed VaultNote model, pure parse/serialize functions for YAML frontmatter + markdown body, and filesystem read/write/list helpers over a vault directory. Document the Dataview-compatible frontmatter conventions inline. Everything domain-agnostic — no medical-specific keys.

successCriteria.description: The project compiles: `npm run build` (tsc) completes with no errors after the src/vault/ module is added.
`npm run typecheck` reports no type errors for the new VaultNote types and parse/serialize/io function signatures.

A unit test parses a fixture note containing string, number, ISO-8601 date, list, and a status key and asserts each frontmatter value is parsed to its documented type and the body equals the text after the closing frontmatter delimiter.
A round-trip unit test asserts serializeNote(parseNote(input)) re-parses to a frontmatter object deep-equal to the original for the documented Dataview conventions.
A unit test writes a VaultNote to a temp dir via writeNote, reads it back via readNote, and asserts equality; listNotes returns every .md file recursively under the vault dir.

nonGoals: Do not write any FactStore / SQLite code in this sprint (deferred to sprint 2).
Do not add a CLI command in this sprint (deferred to sprint 3).
Do not import any module from src/medical/ or hardcode medical frontmatter keys (marker/biomarker/etc.).
Do not call Date.now() or new Date() inside parse/serialize/mapping helpers — these are pure functions.
Do not implement MCP, networking, or SOPS handling in this sprint.
stopConditions: All five success criteria sc-1-1..sc-1-5 pass under the evaluator.
`npm test` passes with the new src/vault/ tests included and no pre-existing tests regressed.
src/vault/ contains the note model, frontmatter parse/serialize, and note-io helpers with no TODO stubs left in the exported functions.
definitionOfDone: A developer can import from src/vault/, read a markdown note from an Obsidian vault into a typed VaultNote { frontmatter, body, path }, edit it, and write it back to disk with the YAML frontmatter and body preserved for the documented Dataview field types. listNotes enumerates every markdown file under a vault directory. The module is domain-agnostic and the new unit tests plus the existing suite are green.
assumptions: Frontmatter is YAML delimited by leading and trailing '---' lines, matching Obsidian/Dataview convention (research §3b lines 148-151).

Parser implementation (a vetted YAML dependency or a dependency-free parser covering the documented scalar/list/date/status conventions) is the generator's choice provided the round-trip tests pass (spec Q4); repo currently has no YAML dependency (package.json lines 64-75).
Documented Dataview conventions cover: string, number, ISO-8601 date, list/array, and a status enum field (active|superseded).
outOfScope: Parsing arbitrarily complex nested YAML beyond the documented Dataview conventions.
Note body markdown parsing/AST (only frontmatter is structured; body is opaque text).
generatorNotes: Create src/vault/types.ts (VaultNote interface: frontmatter: Record<string, unknown>; body: string; path: string), src/vault/frontmatter.ts (parseFrontmatter/serializeFrontmatter — PURE, no clock, no fs), and src/vault/note-io.ts (readNote/writeNote/listNotes — fs + glob). Reuse the existing glob dependency (package.json) for listNotes, the same way other modules enumerate files. Follow the file-header doc-comment style of src/medical/health-store.ts and the import style (node: prefixes, .js extensions on relative imports). Keep parse/serialize PURE (mirror the purity note in src/state/facts.ts:130-135). If hand-rolling YAML, restrict to the documented Dataview conventions and document the restriction; if adding a dependency, pick a small well-maintained YAML parser. Do NOT import anything network-related (src/vault/ is not under the medical ESLint egress fence, but keep it clean). Place fixtures under a test fixtures dir.
evaluatorNotes: Verify round-trip fidelity specifically for a number (e.g. 5.4) staying numeric, an ISO date string staying a parseable date, and a YAML list becoming an array. Confirm the body text after '---' is preserved byte-for-byte including blank lines. Confirm no medical-specific identifiers were introduced (grep src/vault for 'biomarker'|'marker'|'whoop'|'medical'). Confirm parse/serialize do not reference Date.now/new Date. Run the full suite to confirm no regressions.
